blocks.title: What Qargo is and why it works the way it does
blocks.lead: Welcome to Qargo. Before you touch any screen, it helps to know what the system is doing in the background.
blocks.body: Qargo is a Transport Management System built around how transport actually operates. Instead of forcing a workflow on you, it follows the chain your operation already runs: a customer requests a move, the move is broken into physical events at specific addresses, and those events are grouped into something a driver can execute. Knowing this chain before you open any screen is what makes the rest of the app click into place.

blocks.title: Quick glossary
blocks.intro: Four terms you'll hear every day. Expand any row for a one-line definition.

items.a: The commercial promise. What the customer asked for and what they agreed to pay.
A physical event. One pickup or one delivery, at one address, in a time window.
A driver's route. A vehicle, a driver and the stops they'll visit in sequence.
The pricing engine. Customer-specific rules that turn orders into invoice lines.

blocks.title: See Qargo in action
blocks.caption: A short guided tour of the Qargo platform. Click through the steps, then come back to finish the lesson.

blocks.title: Key takeaways
points: Qargo mirrors the real-world chain: Order, then Stop, then Trip.
The system's job is to reflect your operation, not rewrite it.
Orders, Stops, Trips and Rate Cards are the foundation you'll keep returning to.

blocks.title: Quick check
blocks.question: What does an Order capture in Qargo?
options: The commercial promise made to the customer
A single pickup or delivery event
The fuel surcharge applied to a load
blocks.explanation: Orders capture the agreement with the customer. Stops are physical events, Trips are how drivers execute them.
